2019-11-25.title: Across Northern England, Labour’s ‘Red Wall’ Is Showing Cracks
2019-11-25.abstract: As in America, Britain’s rust belt regions are turning to the right, possibly heralding a realignment in next month’s general election.

2019-11-26.title: Rabbi Henry Sobel, 75, Dies; Defied Brazil’s Military Rulers
2019-11-26.abstract: The leader of the largest liberal Jewish congregation in Latin America, he took a stand when a journalist was killed by his torturers in 1975.

2019-11-26.title: 2 Priests Convicted in Argentina of Sexual Abuse of Deaf Children
2019-11-26.abstract: The priests at a now-shuttered school in Mendoza were each sentenced to more than 40 years in prison; a former gardener was sentenced to 18 years.

2019-11-26.title: Taiwan Detains 2 Executives of Firm Accused of Spying for China
2019-11-26.abstract: The executives were detained as officials look into accusations that the company’s workers intervened in Taiwan’s looming national election campaign.

2019-11-26.title: Albania Earthquake Kills at Least 23
2019-11-26.abstract: The 6.4-magnitude quake, one of the strongest to hit the country in decades, struck near Tirana, the capital. Hundreds of injuries were reported.

2019-11-26.title: New Zealand Is Tackling Hot-Button Liberal Issues in One Swoop
2019-11-26.abstract: The country will decide whether to legalize assisted suicide and recreational marijuana, and whether to decriminalize abortion. Conflict-averse New Zealanders may be pushed onto uncomfortable ground.

2019-11-27.title: Iran Strengthens Response to Protests
2019-11-27.abstract: The authorities say “six main elements” of unrest in Tehran were arrested. Secretary of State Mike Pompeo said he had received nearly 20,000 videos and other evidence from Iran of a violent suppression.

2019-11-27.title: Death of Colombian Teenager Drives Protesters Back to Streets
2019-11-27.abstract: Dilan Cruz, 18, died after being struck by a police projectile. Activists call him a symbol of “a generation that was robbed of its rights.”

2019-11-27.title: Japan’s Support for Gay Marriage Is Soaring. But Can It Become Law?
2019-11-27.abstract: The country is being pulled in two directions as it experiences a “boom” in L.G.B.T. awareness but also remains committed to a sometimes inflexible traditional culture.

2019-11-27.title: ‘Evo Morales Is Like a Father to Us’
2019-11-27.abstract: In the coca-growing region where the former Bolivian president got his political start, farmers are loyal to him — and demanding his return.

2019-11-27.title: Amid Death and Debris From Earthquake, Albania Clings to Hope
2019-11-27.abstract: The death toll rose to 31 a day after the Balkan country was struck by one of the strongest tremors there in decades.

2019-11-28.title: Apple, Bowing to Russian Pressure, Recognizes Crimea Annexation on Map
2019-11-28.abstract: The tech giant, following Google and others, has bowed to Russian demands that its apps not show Crimea as belonging to Ukraine on digital maps seen in Russia.

2019-11-28.title: ‘99 Problems but My ABCs Ain’t One’: Jay-Z Sues Over Children’s Book
2019-11-28.abstract: The Little Homie, a small Australian online retailer, says it is celebrating hip-hop. Jay-Z, who has spoken out about black identity and equality, says it is engaging in theft.

2019-11-28.title: North Korea Launches 2 Rockets, South Says
2019-11-28.abstract: The short-range projectiles were launched off the North’s east coast, days after Kim Jong-un ordered artillery fire near a disputed sea border.

2019-11-28.title: Time Is Running Out for Trump’s North Korean Diplomacy, Analysts Say
2019-11-28.abstract: Analysts fear a looming crisis as North Korea warns of a Dec. 31 deadline for a new proposal from the United States on denuclearization.

2019-11-28.title: Roadside Bomb Kills 13 People on Their Way to Afghan Wedding
2019-11-28.abstract: Most of the victims were from a single family. “I am dead, I am finished, I am destroyed,” said one of the survivors.

2019-11-29.title: 7 Killed in Plane Crash in Ontario
2019-11-29.abstract: The authorities in Canada said they were still investigating what caused the plane to crash on Wednesday.

2019-11-29.title: Suddenly, the Chinese Threat to Australia Seems Very Real
2019-11-29.abstract: After a businessman said Chinese agents sought to implant him in Parliament, that revelation and other espionage cases have finally signaled the end of a “let’s get rich together” era.

2019-11-29.title: Espionage and Interference? Australia Grapples With Its China Relationship
2019-11-29.abstract: This talk of Australia this week was China, after revelations that a businessman said Chinese agents sought to implant him in Parliament and other espionage allegations raised questions about foreign interference.

2019-11-29.title: Hong Kong Police End 2-Week Campus Siege
2019-11-29.abstract: The police found no protesters during a final search of Polytechnic University, the site of some of the most violent clashes the city has seen this year.

2019-11-29.title: German Catholic Church Debates Sexuality, Celibacy and Women’s Roles
2019-11-29.abstract: With the German church’s global influence, a meeting of bishops and laypeople to take up hot-button topics has led to warnings of a new schism, originating in the home of the Protestant Reformation.

2019-11-30.title: How an Anti-Brexit London District Could Help Boris Johnson Triumph
2019-11-30.abstract: With a big but divided pro-European vote, one of Labour’s most glamorous election targets risks becoming a symbol of opposition campaign failure.

2019-11-30.title: Spain’s Leftist Outsiders Are on the Verge of Getting Inside
2019-11-30.abstract: Pablo Iglesias and his Unidas Podemos party, who are likely to join a governing coalition for the first time, have had a bumpy ride. What lies ahead could be just as rough.

2019-11-30.title: Students Fainting From Hunger in Venezuela’s Failing School System
2019-11-30.abstract: Children ask teachers at the entrance of one struggling primary school if there is food before deciding whether to come in.

2019-11-30.title: The Lonely and Dangerous Life of the Filipino Seafarer
2019-11-30.abstract: The Philippines for decades has supplied the largest share of labor on the world’s cargo ships. Filipino culture is brought aboard, from food to song and sports.

2019-11-30.title: Iraqi Prime Minister Resigns in Deepening Political Crisis
2019-11-30.abstract: Anger over corruption and Iran’s influence on the country has driven weeks of unrest in Iraq’s streets. But choosing a successor may be a long, arduous task.
